content.problemStatement:
It's well known that our friend Balsa likes the digit 333 very much. He's so obsessed with this digit that he spends time thinking about it even during holidays.

Today, Balsa invented an interesting problem: For a positive integer NNN, find the smallest integer greater than NNN such that its decimal representation contains the digit 333 at least three times.

You are given the integer NNN. Please solve this problem for Balsa.
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the smallest number greater than N containing the digit 333 at least three times.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the smallest number greater than 221 containing the digit 333 at least three times is 333. For the second test case, the smallest number greater than 333 containing the digit 333 at least three times is 1333. For the third test case, the smallest number greater than 3002 containing the digit 333 at least three times is 3033.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can start by checking each number greater than N to see if it contains the digit 333 at least three times. We can do this by converting each number to a string and then checking for the occurrence of '333' in the string. If we find a number that meets the condition, we can return it as the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * 10^9) — in the worst case, we might have to check all numbers up to 2 * 10^9 for each test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the current number and the result
